subject: F1000 Monthly Update - August 2014
body:
To ensure you receive emails from Faculty of 1000, add info@updates.f1000.com to your safelist.

-----------------------------------------------------

Faculty of 1000 |  http://f1000.com                
Monthly Update  |  August 2014 

-----------------------------------------------------
 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~  
 Static research figures brought to life in F1000Research    
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~  
 
A research article published recently in F1000Research could redefine how we think about the 'Figure' in scientific publishing. While traditional static figures can become outdated, in a new article [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-176/ ] by Faculty Member Björn Brembs [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty/member/1941500257244581 ]and Julien Colomb [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty/member/5507936162474982 ], figures 3
[ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-176/v1#f3 ] and 4 [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-176/v1#f4 ] are actually dynamic bundles of code and data that generate images and can be updated through reader interaction or the addition of new data, respectively.

The authors report the finding that not all Drosophila melanogaster control strains are equal, suggesting that results obtained from separate labs may not always be comparable or reproducible. Other researchers can now attempt to replicate the study using a virtual machine provided by the authors and, significantly, add their data to the 'living' figure in the F1000Research article. For more details on these dynamic figures, please read F1000Research [ http://blog.f1000research.com/2014/07/30/bringing-static-research-figures-to-life/ ]'s  and the author's [ http://bjoern.brembs.net/2014/07/the-way-academic-publishing-should-be/ ] blog posts.
 


   F1000Prime                                
==================

945 recommendations  [ http://f1000pri.me/past30days ] added by our Faculty of 10,000+ Members in the past 30 days*. Below is a selection:


3 *
PubMed: academic soothsaying?    
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718463474?key=xLl4N6JKltDwNPK ]   
Employing data from over 25,000 scientists in PubMed, the authors demonstrate that success in academia is apparently predictable.   
van Dijk D, Manor O, Carey LB. Curr Biol. 2014; 24:R516-7  


14 *
Of mice and men   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718363395?key=Z6hCR1z7m4DS6O0 ]   
"This is a very cool article." Mice olfactory exposure to human males during pain studies causes stress-induced analgesia.   
Sorge RE, Martin LJ, Isbester KA et al. Nat Methods. 2014; 11:629-32  


8 *
No more 'Viva la resistance'   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718468934?key=DG8sQrhl54wlt7B ]  
King et al. present a new approach to combat metallo-β-lactamase antibiotic resistance: Aspergillomarasmine A.   
King AM, Reid-Yu SA, Wang W et al. Nature. 2014; 510:503-6  

*Accurate at the time of writing. 


 F1000Prime Reports
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 

Open-access review journal [ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/ ], reporting on the hottest topics.

Advances in managing breast cancer: a clinical update   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/m/6/66/ ]   
Although more sensitive, MRI computer-aided detection of breast cancer is generally considered too expensive for women who are not high risk. However, newer MRI screening protocols are being developed, which cost about the same as ultrasound.  
F1000Prime Report by Ayca Gucalp, Gaorav P. Gupta, Larry Norton [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty/member/499999771097515704 ]   
F1000Prime Rep 2014, 6:66   


 ---------------
 See all recent F1000Prime Reports          
 >> http://f1000.com/prime/reports/                 
 ---------------   
    

----------------------
   

~~~~~~~~~
 Video competition! 
 > http://blog.f1000.com/2014/07/14/celebrating-our-1st-birthday-f1000-specialists/
~~~~~~~~~

Calling F1000 Specialists! Send us a short video of the research in your lab and win a £10 Amazon gift voucher! The best video wins a further £50 voucher. If you'd like to participate but aren't a specialist, join our LinkedIn group [ https://www.linkedin.com/groups?home=&gid=8123808&trk=groups_most_recent-h-logo ].

Find out more on our blog [ http://blog.f1000.com/2014/07/14/celebrating-our-1st-birthday-f1000-specialists/ ]. 


----------------------
  
  
   F1000Research                      
======================

Our innovative open access journal [ http://f1000research.com/ ] offering rapid publication, open peer review and open data.   

Below is a selection of our latest peer reviewed articles:   


OPINION ARTICLE   
CETP inhibitors and cardiovascular disease: Time to think again [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-124/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/3mp ]   
Published: 10 Jun 2014   
Miller NE.   
F1000Research 2014, 3:124   

RESEARCH ARTICLE   
Ecological implications of reduced pollen deposition in alpine plants: a case study using a dominant cushion plant species [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-130/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/3mb ]
Published: 19 Jun 2014  
Reid A, Hooper R, Molenda O et al.   
F1000Research 2014, 3:130   

RESEARCH ARTICLE   
Colonoscopic polyp detection rate is stable throughout the workday including evening colonoscopy sessions [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-107/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/3cf ]   
Published: 13 May 2014   
Thurtle D, Pullinger M, Tsigarides J.   
F1000Research 2014, 3:107   

SOFTWARE TOOL
BridgeDb app: unifying identifier mapping services for Cytoscape [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-148/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/3qb ]   
Published: 01 Jul 2014   
Gao J, Zhang C, van Iersel M et al.   
F1000Research 2014, 3:148  

~~~~~~~~~ 
 New F1000Research article collections
~~~~~~~~~ 

Are you working on rare disease research? Submit your article to our upcoming Rare Diseases article collection [ http://f1000research.com/article-collections/rarediseases ], guest edited by Sean Ekins.  

Next week is Shark Week, and we're marking the occasion with an article collection on Elasmobranch Biology and Conservation [ http://f1000research.com/article-collections/elasmobranch ]. Articles will go live on August 11.



 ---------------------------------------------
  Submit now to F1000Research          
  >> http://f1000research.com/author/submit        
 ----------------------------------------------
 
  
 
    F1000Posters                 
======================
 
Open Repository [ http://f1000.com/posters ] for posters and slide presentations; completely free to view and submit. Below are a few selections:



* PHARMACOLOGY & DRUG DISCOVERY *   

Accurate predictive models of Atomextine's and Citalopraim's effect on motor inhibition in Parkinson's disease. 
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1096208 ]     
Ye Z, Rae C, Nombela C et al.   
F1000Posters 2014, 5:948    


* GENOMICS & GENETICS * 

CNVkit: copy number variant detection and visualization from targeted DNA re-sequencing. 
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1096236 ]   
Talevich E, Shain AH, Bastian BC.   
F1000Posters 2014, 5:920   

 
 ----------------------------------------------
  Deposit now        
  >> http://f1000.com/posters/fordepositors      
 ----------------------------------------------

 * 'Like' us on Facebook *

F1000Research [ https://www.facebook.com/F1000Research ]   
F1000Posters [ https://www.facebook.com/F1000Posters ]  
 
 
 * Connect with us on LinkedIn *  
  
Faculty of 1000 [ https://www.linkedin.com/groups?mostRecent=&gid=1971657 ]   
F1000Research [ https://www.linkedin.com/groups?home=&gid=4267793 ]  
F1000 Specialists [ https://www.linkedin.com/groups?mostRecent=&gid=8123808 ]  
 
 
 * Follow our blogs * 
  
F1000Prime Blog [ http://blog.f1000.com/ ]
F1000Research Blog [ http://blog.f1000research.com/ ]
 
 
----------------------


-----------------------------------------------------   

In addition to this Monthly Update, we also send out emails featuring deals and discounts for registered users. To sign up, registered users should visit MyF1000 via any F1000 product and opt-in to receive "Information from F1000 about special offers" (under Email Alerts).

You have received this message because you asked to receive the F1000 Monthly Update to your registered email address from Faculty of 1000. If you do not wish to receive any further such messages, you may unsubscribe [ http://www.f1000.com/unsubscribe/email?email=jeeproject@yahoo.com&emailAlertType=MONTHLY_UPDATES ] at any time. For further information or enquiries please contact Customer Services at info@f1000.com [ mailto:info@f1000.com ].

Faculty of 1000 Ltd | Science Navigation Group  
Registered in England and Wales with Company Number 3739756     
Middlesex House, 34-42 Cleveland Street | London, W1T 4LB | United Kingdom
